Finance.label: Investment Banking / Private Equity / Asset Management

smart: Financial modeling coursework or CFA Level I prep
grit: Demonstrated deal exposure or client work
Sustained leadership in finance organizations (investment club, consulting club)
build: Financial modeling proof: DCF, LBO, merger model
Deal experience or tombstones (even as intern)

common_gaps.gap: No financial modeling proof on resume
common_gaps.fix: Complete Wall Street Prep or BIWS modeling course and add it. Mention DCF/LBO in project descriptions.
At BB banks, 3.5 is a GPA screen floor. Consider boutique banks or regional shops as alternative entry points.

common_gaps.gap: No finance internship before junior year recruiting
common_gaps.fix: IB recruiting happens in SOPHOMORE year for junior summer. You must start building your pipeline in freshman year.

common_gaps.gap: No investment club or finance organization involvement
common_gaps.fix: Join an investment club and pitch a stock. A real stock pitch adds Build signal that no course can replicate.

firms.name: Goldman Sachs
firms.hiring_culture: Prestige-conscious, GPA and school-sensitive, heavy emphasis on technical modeling and deal knowledge. Known for grueling analyst programs.
firms.notes: SA recruiting is extremely competitive. Network heavily - most offers come through alumni connections.

firms.name: Morgan Stanley
firms.hiring_culture: Similar to GS. Strong wealth management and institutional securities divisions. Values polish and professional presentation.
firms.notes: Wealth Management has a different (more accessible) recruiting pathway than IBD.

firms.name: JP Morgan
firms.hiring_culture: Largest bank by assets. More volume in recruiting means slightly more accessible than pure GS/MS/Lazard. Consumer banking track for non-IBD roles.
firms.notes: JPM has the broadest set of entry-level programs across IBD, Asset Management, and Markets.

firms.name: Lazard / Evercore / Centerview
firms.hiring_culture: Smaller class, higher deal responsibility, less bureaucracy than BB. Pays more per analyst than most BB banks.
firms.notes: Elite boutiques are as selective as GS/MS. Strong relationships with PE firms.

firms.name: Blackstone / KKR / Apollo
firms.hiring_culture: PE typically recruits from 1st-year IB analysts. GPA and bank brand are critical filters. Near-perfect track record required.
firms.notes: Direct PE recruiting from undergrad is rare. Path is: top school → BB or EB bank → PE.

smart: Any major but GPA is critical (3.7+ for MBB)
grit: Leadership in case clubs or business organizations
build: Case competition wins or top placements
Data analysis or strategy project with quantified outcome

common_gaps.fix: Join your school's case club. Win or place in a case competition. These are the best resume signals for consulting recruiting.

common_gaps.gap: Behavioral answers not structured using STAR or similar framework
common_gaps.fix: MBB behavioral interviews are rigorous. Prepare 8-10 polished stories. PEI (Personal Experience Interview) is formalized at McKinsey.
Tier 2 firms (Deloitte S&O, EY-Parthenon, Oliver Wyman) are accessible at 3.5+. Use as a bridge to MBB through lateral transfer.

common_gaps.gap: No quant or analytical project to anchor Build signal
common_gaps.fix: Add a data-driven project: market sizing analysis, financial model, or strategy analysis. Even a class project with methodology described works.

firms.name: McKinsey & Company
firms.hiring_culture: Most structured of MBB. Personal Experience Interview (PEI) is a formalized behavioral test. Recruits from target schools heavily.
firms.notes: Solve problems at scale. McKinsey values intellectual rigor and structured thinking above all.

firms.name: Boston Consulting Group (BCG)
firms.hiring_culture: More creative and entrepreneurial than McKinsey. Values innovation and new ideas. Case interviews are similar difficulty.
firms.notes: BCG is building out its AI and digital practice aggressively - tech + consulting backgrounds are differentiating.

firms.name: Bain & Company
firms.hiring_culture: Most collegial culture of MBB. PE-focused consulting work. Known for the best analyst training program.
firms.notes: Bain analysts have high placement into PE. The culture is slightly more team-oriented than McKinsey.

firms.name: Deloitte S&O / Monitor
firms.hiring_culture: Lower bar than MBB but excellent strategy work. Good entry point for candidates targeting MBB lateral.
firms.notes: Deloitte S&O is formally separate from Deloitte Consulting and maintains MBB-adjacent culture.

firms.name: Oliver Wyman
firms.hiring_culture: Strong in financial services consulting. Known for analytical rigor. Smaller firm means faster growth.
firms.notes: If targeting finance or insurance industry consulting, Oliver Wyman is a top-5 choice.

Marketing.label: Brand, Digital Marketing, and Product Marketing

build: Portfolio of campaigns, copy, or creative work

common_gaps.gap: No campaign portfolio or metrics to show
common_gaps.fix: Run a real campaign - even for a student org. Measure results. Add numbers: reach, conversions, follower growth, engagement rate.
Google Analytics, Meta Blueprint, and HubSpot certifications are free and widely recognized. Add them under Skills or Certifications.
